methodsAttempted.query: site:deakin.edu.au generative AI policy students assessment Deakin University

methodsAttempted.notes: Confirmed official student and research pages; Deakin Life blog results were treated as lower-priority context and not used as evidence.

methodsAttempted.query: site:deakin.edu.au Deakin University AI tools privacy data generative AI
methodsAttempted.notes: Found the official Deakin genAI overview page and approved genAI tools for learning page.
Followed official links from Deakin's genAI overview and student guidance pages; staff SharePoint content requiring login was rejected.

artifacts.summary: Official Deakin discovery located public pages covering institution-level genAI principles, approved learning tools, student study and assessment guidance, acknowledgement guidance, and research integrity guidance. Firecrawl was used for search and page extraction because direct curl requests to www.deakin.edu.au returned Cloudflare challenge responses.

targets.sourceTitle: Using genAI for study and assessments

stopConditions: Stop after public official Deakin pages provide substantive source text for institutional, student, approved-tool, and research guidance.
Do not bypass staff SharePoint login or Cloudflare challenge responses encountered by direct curl.

artifacts.errorReason: Login-required staff SharePoint resource linked from Deakin public page; no bypass attempted.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Using genAI for study and assessments

artifacts.claimText: Deakin has institution-level genAI principles and a genAI Steering Group coordinating guidance across learning and teaching, research, enterprise use, and digital services.
artifacts.normalizedValue: institutional genAI principles and steering group

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Deakin's genAI Steering Group was established in 2023 to: oversee the uptake of genAI tools and associated activity; coordinate institutional guidelines for the use of genAI, with cross-reference to specific guidance for learning and teaching, research and enterprise use; consolidate advice from relevant teams in the Academic and Research and Innovation Portfolios and Digital Services on current practice, emerging issues and technologies.
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: Deakin says its genAI Steering Group coordinates institutional guidelines and connects learning and teaching, research, enterprise, and digital-services advice.
artifacts.rightsNote: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.notes: Approved as a narrow institutional governance and principles claim. It does not assert binding policy status beyond the cited overview page.

artifacts.claimText: Deakin identifies a set of approved genAI and digital learning tools for learning, including Deakin GEM, Studiosity+, Microsoft Copilot for web, and FeedbackFruits.
artifacts.normalizedValue: approved learning tools: Deakin GEM, Studiosity+, Microsoft Copilot for web, FeedbackFruits

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Deakin provides a set of endorsed generative AI (genAI) and digital learning tools to support teaching and learning in ways that are safe, ethical, and educationally sound. These tools have been formally evaluated and approved to align with Deakin's academic values, privacy and security obligations, and learning and teaching goals.
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: Deakin lists endorsed genAI and digital learning tools that it says have been formally evaluated and approved for learning contexts.
artifacts.rightsNote: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.notes: Approved as an approved-tools claim scoped to the student learning page and named tools listed there.

artifacts.claimText: Deakin student guidance says genAI may be used as a starting point for some study tasks, but not to write the final assessment or do the work being assessed.
artifacts.normalizedValue: students may use genAI supportively but remain responsible for own assessment work

citation.citationTitle: Using genAI for study and assessments
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Using genAI for study and assessments
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: You might use genAI as a starting point for writing (for example, a writing template) but never to write your final assessment. Never use genAI to do the work that you are being assessed on. It is your responsibility to write and create your own assessments.
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: Deakin tells students genAI can be a starting point for writing support, but not a way to write the final assessment or complete the assessed work.
artifacts.rightsNote: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

citation.citationTitle: Using genAI for study and assessments
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.notes: Approved as a student assessment integrity claim using Deakin's own wording around final assessment and assessed work.

artifacts.claimText: Deakin guidance says students should acknowledge genAI use where it contributed to developing assessment work and should include tool, access date, prompts, output, and where it was used.
artifacts.normalizedValue: acknowledge genAI use in assessment development

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Where you have used generative AI (genAI) in developing your assessment (for example, in the development of ideas, problem solving, data analysis, significant writing feedback) you should acknowledge your use of genAI. It is essential that you provide details about where and how you have used it.
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: Deakin says students should acknowledge genAI use in assessment development and explain where and how it was used.
artifacts.rightsNote: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.notes: Approved as an acknowledgement claim scoped to the student assessment acknowledgement page.

artifacts.claimText: Deakin research guidance says commercial genAI platforms may store data externally and researchers should use only non-sensitive, non-confidential content suitable for public or external use.
artifacts.normalizedValue: external AI tools restricted to non-sensitive, non-confidential research content

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Commercial generative AI platforms like ChatGPT, Bing and others store data externally. Once entered, your information may be retained, reused or accessed beyond your control, even if the platform offers options to delete or restrict access. To minimise risk, use only non-sensitive, non-confidential content that is suitable for public or external use.
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: Deakin warns that commercial genAI platforms can store data externally and tells researchers to use only non-sensitive, non-confidential content suitable for public or external use.
artifacts.rightsNote: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.notes: Approved as a research privacy/data-handling claim scoped to commercial external AI tools.

artifacts.claimText: Deakin research guidance says HDR thesis use of generative AI is limited to copyediting and proofreading, with generated images allowed only where part of an approved methodology and fully disclosed.
artifacts.normalizedValue: HDR thesis AI use limited to copyediting/proofreading except approved disclosed methodology for image generation

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Generative AI may be used for copyediting and proofreading thesis text only. Refer to the Institute for Professional Editors guidelines for what qualifies as copyediting and proofreading support. Using generative AI to create or manipulate images is not permitted in your thesis, unless image generation is part of your approved research methodology and fully disclosed.
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: For HDR thesis writing, Deakin limits generative AI to copyediting and proofreading, with image generation allowed only when it is part of approved methodology and disclosed.
artifacts.evidenceLocator: Using generative AI in HDR thesis writing
artifacts.rightsNote: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.notes: Approved as a narrow HDR thesis research-use claim; it does not infer rules for all coursework or all research outputs.

artifacts.title: Deakin University genAI policy source review
artifacts.summary: Deakin public official pages provide institution-level genAI principles, a steering-group coordination page, approved learning-tool guidance, student assessment and acknowledgement guidance, and research integrity guidance on data handling and HDR thesis use. Staff-only SharePoint guidance was linked from the public overview but not accessed.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.
Direct curl requests to www.deakin.edu.au returned Cloudflare challenge responses; Firecrawl successfully retrieved the public pages used for evidence.
Staff SharePoint guidance linked from Deakin's public genAI page requires login and was not fetched or used as evidence.
Claims are scoped to the cited official pages and do not infer course-level permissions beyond Deakin's instruction to check unit or unit-chair guidance.
artifacts.sourceRightsPolicy: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.
